parentQuestion: The serous membrane covering the abdominal organs is called ___.

scaffolds.q: SEROUS membranes are thin, slippery membranes that line body cavities.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! They produce watery fluid that reduces friction.

scaffolds.q: Serous membranes have TWO layers: parietal and visceral.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Parietal = wall. Visceral = organ. Two sides of the same membrane.

scaffolds.q: PARIETAL layer lines the WALL of the cavity.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Parietal = wall. Think of it as wallpaper lining the cavity.

scaffolds.q: VISCERAL layer covers the ORGANS themselves.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Visceral = organs. Like shrink-wrap on the organ surface.

scaffolds.q: The PERITONEUM is the serous membrane of the abdominal cavity.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Pleura = lungs. Pericardium = heart. Peritoneum = abdomen.

scaffolds.q: VISCERAL peritoneum covers the abdominal ORGANS.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! The layer directly touching stomach, intestines, liver, etc.

scaffolds.q: PARIETAL peritoneum lines the abdominal WALL (not the organs).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The layer touching the inside of your abdominal wall.

scaffolds.q: The membrane covering abdominal ORGANS = VISCERAL peritoneum.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Visceral = organ-covering. Peritoneum = abdominal membrane.

scaffolds.q: The answer is VISCERAL PERITONEUM - covers abdominal organs.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not parietal (that's the wall), not mesentery (that's a double layer).
